executive_summary.one_paragraph: CLDN18 (principally isoform CLDN18.2) is a multi-pass transmembrane tight-junction protein with high, direct-multi-method evidence for surface accessibility on tumor cells. Live-cell flow cytometry on intact cancer cells, 89Zr-zolbetuximab PET in xenografts, and membranous IHC across multiple tumor cohorts converge on confirmed ECD exposure. Surface accessibility is strongly state-dependent: in normal differentiated gastric mucosa the ECD is junctionally sequestered; malignant transformation disrupts tight junctions and exposes the ECD. An FDA-approved antibody (zolbetuximab) directly validates the therapeutically accessible surface form. Key risks are junctional subdomain sequestration in normal tissue and uncertain localization of the CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein in a minority of tumors.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: CLDN18 surface accessibility is supported by multiple independent direct methods from multiple sources. (1) Live-cell flow cytometry on intact N87 cells ectopically expressing CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 (PMC11712040); (2) Live-cell FACS binding assays with anti-CLDN18.2 antibodies on intact tumor cells, explicitly no permeabilization (PMC12323529, PMC12808354, PMID19047087); (3) In vivo functional imaging: 89Zr-zolbetuximab PET in xenograft models showing specific tumor uptake significantly above IgG control, directly confirming ECD accessibility in vivo (PMC12766881); (4) Membranous IHC (non-permeabilized, membranous-only scoring) across multiple tumor cohorts (PMC12018027, PMC11979426, PMC12078809, PMC12975664). Two minor ambiguities exist: IHC scoring guidelines for 1+ vs 2+ are noted as imprecise, and the CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein's surface localization is uncertain. These are minor caveats that do not undermine the robust multi-method direct evidence for wildtype CLDN18.2 surface accessibility.

antibodies.name: pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Pan-specific antibody recognizing both CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 isoforms; no cross-reactivity exclusion stated for this reagent.

expression_observations.context: N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2; surface expression of both isoforms detected by live-cell flow cytometry

antibodies.name: hVH6 anti-CLDN18.2 antibody
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Cross-reactivity with recombinant CLDN18.1 was systematically ruled out at each development stage via counter-screening, confirming CLDN18.2 isoform specificity.

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody (isoform-selective)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Antibody does not cross-react with CLDN18.1 lung-specific splice variant; isoform specificity confirmed by counter-screening.

expression_observations.context: Cancer cells (epithelial tumor cell lines) expressing endogenous CLDN18.2; surface staining positive, confirming plasma-membrane accessibility of CLDN18.2 ECD
CLDN18 identified in Cell Surface Protein Atlas (mass spectrometry surfaceome database) as a cell-surface gene in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma context
Gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma tumors eligible for zolbetuximab therapy; moderate-to-strong membranous IHC staining in ≥75% of tumor cells required for eligibility
Biliary tract cancer (BTC) samples (n=237); CLDN18 expression in 29.5% and CLDN18 positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous staining ≥75% of tumor cells) in 5.5% of BTCs

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody (membranous-only scoring)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Only membranous staining considered positive; cytoplasmic signal excluded.

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18 antibody clone 43-14A
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: IHC scoring of 1+ vs 2+ membranous staining noted as not well described; cases with 2+/3+ staining in 40-75% range required careful consideration.

expression_observations.context: PDAC cohort; cases with 2+ or 3+ membranous CLDN18 staining in the 40-75% range identified as scoring-ambiguous by pathologists

antibodies.name: zolbetuximab (89Zr-labeled, anti-CLDN18.2)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Isoform-selective anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody; FDA-approved for gastric/GEJ cancer targeting CLDN18.2 ECD. Control IgG used to confirm specificity of tumor uptake.

expression_observations.context: GSU xenograft: mean tumor uptake 24.4 ± 7.8 %ID/g on day 6 with 89Zr-zolbetuximab vs 4.8 ± 1.9 %ID/g control IgG (P=0.0029)
PATU8988S xenograft: mean tumor uptake 36.6 ± 10.1 %ID/g on day 6 with 89Zr-zolbetuximab
HUPT-4 xenograft: mean tumor uptake 16.48 ± 4.7 %ID/g on day 6 with 89Zr-zolbetuximab

non_surface_expression.context: Human PDAC versus noncancerous pancreas tissue — RNA sequencing showing significantly higher CLDN18.2 mRNA in PDAC with no significant difference between treated and untreated tumors
Biliary tract cancer (BTC) primary tumor samples — IHC membranous staining showing CLDN18 expression in 29.5% and positivity (≥75% moderate-to-strong membranous) in 5.5% of 237 samples
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma primary tissue — IHC membranous staining data from PDAC cohort including scoring challenge observations with 43-14A clone

Gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma tumor tissue — IHC membranous staining used as clinical trial eligibility criterion (≥75% tumor cells moderate-to-strong membranous, SPOTLIGHT/GLOW trials)

therapeutic_engagement.description: Zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody, FDA-approved for gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma) is the lead therapeutic engaging CLDN18.2 at the cell surface. Clinical eligibility requires ≥75% of tumor cells with moderate-to-strong membranous IHC staining (SPOTLIGHT/GLOW trial criteria). Additional programs include CAR-T therapies targeting surface CLDN18.2, and preclinical radiolabeled formats (89Zr/177Lu-zolbetuximab) for PET imaging and radioligand therapy in gastric and PDAC xenograft models. Multiple isoform-selective anti-CLDN18.2 antibodies (e.g., hVH6) have been developed with counter-screening against CLDN18.1.
therapeutic_engagement.surface_form_rationale: CLDN18 is a multi-pass transmembrane tight-junction protein with no documented secreted or shed isoform in this ledger. All therapeutics engage the membrane-anchored form. Importantly, in normal differentiated gastric epithelium CLDN18.2's extracellular domain is buried within tight junctions and inaccessible; tumor disruption of tight junctions exposes the ECD at the free cell surface, enabling antibody and CAR-T engagement. Only the membrane-anchored surface form is relevant for therapeutic targeting.

contradicting_evidence.claim: IHC scoring guidelines distinguishing CLDN18 1+ from 2+ membranous staining are described as 'not well described', introducing a methodological ambiguity that undermines the reliability of surface-staining quantitation in clinical and research settings. This makes it difficult to confidently interpret CLDN18 surface-accessibility data derived from IHC studies.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: This is a quantitation and inter-pathologist reproducibility issue rather than evidence that CLDN18 is absent from the cell surface; the protein is still membrane-localised, but the degree of surface expression is harder to stratify accurately. Standardisation of scoring criteria would resolve the ambiguity.

contradicting_evidence.claim: In tumours harbouring the CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion, the subcellular localisation of the resulting fusion protein is uncertain. Because surface expression of CLDN18 is a prerequisite for efficacy of zolbetuximab and CLDN18.2-directed CAR-T therapies, this uncertainty constitutes an isoform-level contradiction for the surface-accessibility hypothesis in fusion-positive cancers.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: The ARHGAP26 fusion partner may disrupt the normal trafficking or membrane-insertion of CLDN18, potentially retaining the fusion protein intracellularly or mislocalising it. This would apply only to the minority of tumours carrying this specific fusion and not to canonical CLDN18.2-expressing cancers.

tissues.tissue: small bowel (CD-type borderline neoplasm / intestinal metaplasia)
cell_types: CD-SBN lesion cells with gastric phenotype

cell_types.cell_type: N87 gastric cancer cells (ectopic expression model)
gastric cancer tumor cells with CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion
adenocarcinoma of the esophagogastric junction (AEG/GEJ) tumor cells
intestinal metaplasia lesion cells (CD-SBN, gastric phenotype)

anatomical_accessibility.context: gastric mucosa epithelium (normal and gastric cancer)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: CLDN18.2 is a tight junction constituent in gastric epithelial cells (HPA cell junctions annotation). In normal polarized gastric mucosa, tight junction proteins are sequestered at the apicolateral belt, limiting systemic antibody access. However, in gastric cancer, junction disruption can expose epitopes.

CLDN18.1 localizes to tight junctions of alveolar epithelial cells. Tight junction belts are sequestered at the apicolateral interface of the air-blood barrier; systemic delivery cannot access the luminal/apical face without crossing the epithelial barrier.

anatomical_accessibility.context: gastric cancer and ectopic tumor contexts (PDAC, esophageal, ovarian, lung tumors)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: Malignant transformation frequently disrupts tight junctions, potentially relocating CLDN18.2 to a less restricted membrane domain accessible from the interstitium. Flow cytometry of intact cells confirms surface-accessible extracellular domains, but residual junctional sequestration varies by tumor architecture.

anatomical_accessibility.context: CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein in gastric cancer
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: The chromosomal rearrangement creating the CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion may alter normal tight junction targeting of the CLDN18 extracellular domains. Whether the fusion protein traffics to the cell surface in a therapeutically accessible orientation is unresolved.

accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.1 isoform expression is restricted to lung alveolar epithelial cells, driven by NKX2.1 transcription factor; absent from non-lung tissues. Surface tight-junction localization is confined to this lineage.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.1 extracellular domains are surface-accessible specifically at alveolar epithelial tight junctions; binders targeting CLDN18.1 face on-target normal lung tissue exposure.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Differentiated gastric mucosal epithelial cells (normal stomach)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 isoform surface expression is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa in normal tissue; absent from gastric stem cell zone and all other normal tissues.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 extracellular domains are surface-accessible only at gastric mucosal tight junctions in normal tissue, making it an attractive target with narrow normal-tissue on-target toxicity risk.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal non-gastric tissues (pancreas, esophagus, ovary, lung) lacking CLDN18.2 expression
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors with ectopic CLDN18.2 activation
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is ectopically activated in multiple tumor types outside its normal gastric restriction upon malignant transformation, with substantial prevalence in gastric (54.5%), pancreatic (20.7%), biliary (21.7%), and other cancers by IHC.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 extracellular epitopes become surface-accessible on tumor cells across multiple cancer types, substantially expanding the population of patients whose tumors can be targeted by anti-CLDN18.2 agents.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal gastric mucosa with surface-accessible CLDN18.2 on differentiated epithelial cells
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is retained upon malignant transformation of gastric epithelium and remains expressed in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and their metastases, unlike many tight junction proteins that are lost in cancer.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface CLDN18.2 accessibility is preserved in gastric cancer, meaning therapeutic antibodies (e.g. zolbetuximab) and CAR-T cells can engage CLDN18.2 on both primary and metastatic gastric tumor cells.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal alveolar epithelium consistently expressing CLDN18.1 at tight junctions
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.1 expression is completely absent in 83% of NM-LUAD resections and 84.8% of biopsies; even lepidic (less aggressive) NM-LUAD components show low expression in 24/26 cases. This represents near-total loss of CLDN18 in this tumor subtype.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Despite normal alveolar surface accessibility, CLDN18.1 is lost in the vast majority of NM-LUAD tumors, severely limiting surface target availability for CLDN18-directed therapies in this lung cancer subtype.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal endocervical tissue lacking CLDN18 expression
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Gastric-type adenocarcinoma (GAS) of the uterine cervix
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18 positivity detected in 92% of gastric-type endocervical adenocarcinomas (5% cut-off) and 33% at stringent ≥75% tumor cell threshold, versus 0% in non-gastric subtypes and only 2-7% in HPV-associated ECAs.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 surface accessibility is strongly induced in gastric-type cervical adenocarcinoma, a rare but distinct histological subtype, offering a surface target for CLDN18.2-directed therapeutics in this specific tumor class.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal biliary tract epithelium lacking CLDN18 expression
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18 expression (any staining) appears in 29.5% of BTC samples; stringent membranous positivity (moderate-to-strong in ≥75% tumor cells) in 5.5% (13/237), indicating low but non-zero disease-state-induced surface presence.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: A minority of BTC patients have sufficient surface CLDN18 to be eligible for CLDN18-directed therapy; the low prevalence at stringent thresholds limits the overall accessible patient population.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal ovarian tissue lacking CLDN18 expression
accessibility_modulation.change: Transcriptomic analysis identifies CLDN18 as having the highest differential expression between ovarian tumors and normal ovarian tissue among adhesion proteins, indicating strong ectopic upregulation upon malignant transformation.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18 extracellular domains become surface-accessible on ovarian tumor cells, creating a targetable surface epitope absent from normal ovarian tissue and enabling ovarian cancer as a potential indication for CLDN18-directed agents.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Adenocarcinoma of the esophagogastric junction (AEG/GEJ tumors)
accessibility_modulation.change: Surfaceome proteomics shows measurable log2 fold-change increases in CLDN18 membrane protein abundance in AEG/GEJ tumor versus paired normal adjacent tissue, confirming disease-state-induced surface upregulation.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18 membrane epitopes are more accessible in GEJ adenocarcinoma than in paired normal tissue, supporting anti-CLDN18 ADC or antibody approaches in this tumor type.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Gastric cancer cells with intact CLDN18.2 surface expression at tight junctions
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Gastric cancer cells harboring CLDN18-ARHGAP26 chromosomal gene fusion
accessibility_modulation.change: The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion rearrangement creates an anomalous fusion protein whose subcellular localization is uncertain; it is unknown whether the CLDN18 extracellular domains remain surface-accessible or are misdirected intracellularly.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Tumors with CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion may lose surface-accessible CLDN18 extracellular epitopes, potentially rendering them resistant to zolbetuximab or CAR-T therapies that require cell-surface CLDN18 expression.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Gastric stem cell zone (undifferentiated progenitor cells)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 surface expression is absent from the gastric stem cell zone and only appears upon terminal differentiation of gastric mucosal epithelial cells, establishing a differentiation-state gate on surface expression.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 surface accessibility in normal stomach is gated by differentiation status; stem/progenitor cells lack the target, reducing risk of targeting undifferentiated gastric precursors while confirming accessibility on mature epithelium.

structure.source: AlphaFold DB (STUB — fetchers not yet built)

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: No evidence in the merged ledger identifies a required co-receptor or trafficking partner for CLDN18 plasma-membrane insertion. Claudins oligomerize laterally with other claudins at tight junctions, but individual isoforms traffic to the membrane independently. Live-cell flow cytometry detects surface ECD in ectopic overexpression and endogenous contexts without any documented co-receptor requirement.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: In normal polarized gastric and alveolar epithelium, CLDN18 ECDs are sequestered within the tight-junction belt at the apicolateral interface, physically inaccessible to systemically delivered antibodies. In tumor tissue, malignant disruption of tight junctions relocates CLDN18 to a freely accessible plasma-membrane domain, as confirmed by live-cell flow and in vivo PET imaging. Severity is moderate because the restriction is overcome in the therapeutic disease context but persists as an on-target normal-tissue accessibility limit.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: CLDN18 is a 4-transmembrane claudin family protein with two short extracellular loops (EL1 ~50 aa, EL2 ~20 aa). The total extracellular surface presented is small. Despite this, isoform-selective monoclonal antibodies and zolbetuximab engage the ECD with sufficient affinity for FDA-approved therapeutic activity, confirming that the small ECD is nonetheless druggable.

epitope_masking.rationale: In normal polarized epithelium the EL2 loop, the primary CLDN18.2 isoform-selective epitope, is buried within the tight-junction strand complex through cis- and trans-claudin interactions, constituting conformational and partner-mediated masking. Tumor-associated junction disruption reduces this masking and is the biological basis for tumor-selective therapeutic targeting. No glycan masking is documented in the ledger.

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 encodes a protein with amino acid sequence similar to the claudin multigene family, which constitutes tight junction strands in epithelial cells, establishing it as a multi-pass transmembrane tight-junction protein with extracellular-facing loops accessible at the cell surface.

spans.quote: The gene product exhibits an amino acid sequence similar to those of the claudin multigene family of proteins that constitute tight junction strands in epithelial cells.

evidence.claim: HPA database annotates CLDN18 subcellular location as Cell Junctions, consistent with tight-junction plasma-membrane localization and extracellular accessibility.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Cell Junctions.

evidence.claim: A target-evaluation table derived from public genomic databases annotates CLDN18 as a 'Membrane'-localized protein (HPA mean: 64.4, tumor mean: 133.3, tumor/HPA ratio: 2.1), confirming plasma-membrane topology and supporting surface accessibility in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma relative to normal tissue.

spans.quote: crystal structure CEACAM5 Membrane 353.2 257.8 0.7 ND ND Low Yes MUCL3 Membrane/cytoplasm 152.0 57.8 0.4 ND ND ND No TMPRSS4 Membrane/intracellular 131.0 72.1 0.6 Medium Medium Low No COL17A1 Cell junction, desmosome, membrane 96.8 78.4 0.8 ND ND ND No MSLN Membrane 67.5 272.7 4.0 ND ND ND No CLDN18 Membrane 64.4 133.3 2.1 ND ND ND No PSCA Membrane 51.8 135.2 2.6 Low ND ND No AQP5 Membrane, cytoplasmic vesicle membrane 37.9 68.9 1.8 Low Low Low Yes VSIG2 Membrane 18.9 159.0 8.4 High High Medium No FXYD3 Membrane 17.4 236.8 13.6 ND Medium ND No GJB3 Membrane

evidence.claim: Computational/genomic analysis identifies CLDN18 as an adhesion protein with extracellular antigenic epitopes, grouping it with surface-accessible targets showing high differential expression between ovarian tumors and normal tissues, supporting ECD accessibility on tumor cell surfaces.

spans.quote: The final set of genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with the highest difference between ovarian tumors and normal tissues consisted of membrane receptors (TFRC, OPRK1, EBAG9, HTR5A, GABRB1, and CD47), adhesion proteins (EpCAM, CLDN18, and CLDN1), and molecule transporters (KCNG1, KCNH2, and SLC22A11) ( Figure 1E ).

evidence.claim: Live-cell flow cytometry of N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody recognizing both isoforms demonstrates surface expression of both splice variants; no permeabilization, intact cells, confirming plasma-membrane accessibility of CLDN18 ECD.

spans.quote: Left, Flow cytometry analysis of CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 surface expression in N87 cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody recognizing both isoforms.

evidence.claim: During development of hVH6 (anti-CLDN18.2 antibody), cross-reactivity with recombinant CLDN18.1 was systematically ruled out at each development stage via counter-screening, establishing isoform specificity and supporting the reliability of CLDN18.2-targeted surface-engagement assays.

spans.quote: Due to the close homology of CLDN18.2 with CLDN18.1, potential cross-reactivity with recombinant CLDN18.1 was ruled out at each stage of hVH6 development through counter-screening against recombinant CLDN18.1.

evidence.claim: Live-cell FACS-based antibody binding assay used to assess surface engagement of anti-CLDN18.2 antibodies: serial dilutions of test antibodies incubated with intact target cells in FACS staining buffer, followed by secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher Scientific, RRID:AB_2535862) staining and live/dead exclusion; method family is flow cytometry, permeabilization absent, confirming surface-binding readout.

spans.quote: For cell binding assays, serial dilutions of test and control antibodies were incubated with target cells in FACS staining buffer, starting at a concentration of 1 mM and diluted fourfold across 10 points, followed by washing with FACS staining buffer and staining with secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher Scientific Cat# A-21445, RRID:AB_2535862) at a final concentration of 4 ug/mL along with live/dead red fixable reagent (ThermoFisher) at 1/1000 in FACS staining buffer for 1 hour at 4°C.

evidence.claim: Authors explicitly describe FACS as a method to directly measure antibody binding to cell-surface proteins in the context of screening anti-CLAUDIN 18.2 antibodies targeting the protein on the cell membrane, confirming the flow-cytometry surface-binding paradigm for CLDN18.2 ECD accessibility.

spans.quote: FACS allows us to directly measure the binding of antibodies to cell surface proteins, which is highly relevant to our goal of screening antibodies that specifically target CLAUDIN 18.2 on the cell membrane.

evidence.claim: Monoclonal antibodies raised against CLDN18.2 bind the antigen on the surface of cancer cells and do not cross-react with the lung-specific splice variant CLDN18.1, demonstrating isoform-selective surface accessibility of CLDN18.2 ECD in tumor cells; qualifies CLDN18.2 as a pan-cancer antibody therapy target at the cell surface.

spans.quote:
Most importantly, we were able to raise monoclonal antibodies that bind to CLDN18.2 but not to its lung-specific splice variant and recognize the antigen on the surface of cancer cells.

Conclusions

Its highly restricted expression pattern in normal tissues, its frequent ectopic activation in a diversity of human cancers, and the ability to specifically target this molecule at the cell surface of tumor cells qualify CLDN18.2 as a novel, highly attractive pan-cancer target for the antibody therapy of epithelial tumors.

evidence.claim: Integrative genomic study of pancreatic cancer used the Cell Surface Protein Atlas (mass spectrometry surfaceome database) to identify and filter candidate cell-surface genes, and CLDN18 was among the cell-surface gene set considered, providing mass-spectrometry-database-level evidence for cell-surface localization.

spans.quote: Cell surface genes considered for analysis were taken from lists derived from predictions from mass spectrometry data (Cell Surface Protein Atlas 103 ).

evidence.claim: Clinical trial eligibility criteria for zolbetuximab-based therapy require tumors to express CLDN18 with moderate-to-strong membranous IHC staining in ≥75% of tumor cells, establishing that CLDN18 surface expression detectable by IHC is the functional threshold for therapeutic targeting.

spans.quote: Eligible patients had tumors expressing CLDN18, with moderate to strong membranous expression by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in ≥75 % tumor cells[ 16 , 17 ].

evidence.claim: IHC analysis of 237 biliary tract cancer (BTC) samples shows CLDN18 expression in 29.5% and CLDN18 positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous staining in ≥75% of tumor cells) in 5.5%, confirming membranous surface presence of CLDN18 protein in a subset of BTCs, with the membranous pattern serving as the surface-accessibility readout.

spans.quote: Overall, CLDN18 expression was observed in 70/237 BTCs (29.5%) samples, while CLDN18 positivity (i.e. moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of the tumor cells) was found in 13/237 BTCs (5.5%).

evidence.claim: IHC surface-staining scoring method for CLDN18: tumor positivity defined as ≥75% of tumor cells with moderate-to-strong membranous staining, explicitly restricted to membranous pattern; method follows SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trial criteria, providing the surface-accessibility scoring framework for CLDN18 IHC.

spans.quote:
Tumor positivity for CLDN18 was determined if ≥75% of tumor cells exhibited moderate-to-strong membranous staining, following the evaluation methods used in the SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trials. 10 , 11 , 18
The presence and pattern of intratumoral heterogeneity was assessed only in cases with CLDN18 positivity, as weak staining makes it difficult to assess staining patterns.

evidence.claim: IHC homogeneity scoring for CLDN18: absence of intratumoral heterogeneity defined as CLDN18 expressed in >90% of area with moderate-to-strong membranous staining, providing a refined surface-staining quantitation criterion for surface-accessibility assessment.

spans.quote: The absence of intratumoral heterogeneity (i.e. homogenous pattern of expression of CLDN18) was defined as CLDN18 being expressed in >90% of the area with a moderate-to-strong membranous staining.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring method explicitly restricts positivity to membranous staining pattern only, excluding cytoplasmic signal, directly anchoring the surface-accessibility interpretation of CLDN18 IHC in this cohort.

spans.quote: Only membranous staining was considered positive.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring rubric for CLDN18 membranous staining: percentage of tumor cells with any membranous staining scored on a 0–5+ scale (0%→0; 1–4%→1+; 5–9%→2+; 10–24%→3+; 25–74%→4+; ≥75%→5+), providing quantitative surface-staining resolution for surface-accessibility annotation.

spans.quote: The percentage of tumour cells with any membranous staining for CLDN18 was scored as follows: score 0, 0%; score 1+, 1%–4%; score 2+, 5%–9%; score 3+, 10%–24%; score 4+, 25%–74%; score 5+, ≥75%.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring challenge noted in PDAC cohort using CLDN18/43-14A antibody clone: cases with 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in 40–75% range required careful consideration; identifies antibody clone 43-14A and highlights the difficulty of IHC scoring precision as a methodological caveat for surface-accessibility quantitation.

spans.quote: With CLDN18/43–14A staining in our PDAC cohort, we found that cases exhibiting 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in the 40–75 % range required more careful consideration for scoring.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring guidelines for CLDN18 1+ vs 2+ membranous staining are described as 'not well described', flagging a methodological ambiguity in surface-staining quantitation that constitutes a caveat for interpreting CLDN18 IHC surface-accessibility data.

spans.quote: Another challenge with the pathologic interpretation of CLDN18 IHC is that guidelines defining 1+ vs. 2+ membranous staining are not well described.

evidence.claim: Review/clinical study characterizes CLDN18.2 as a tight-junction protein 'overexpressed and differently exposed' in gastric cancer, gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, and PDAC, indicating that in normal differentiated gastric epithelium CLDN18.2 ECD is inaccessible (buried in tight junctions), while in tumors the ECD becomes exposed at the cell surface — a key surface-accessibility distinction for therapeutic targeting.

cell_context.disease_state: gastric cancer, gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma

spans.quote: Claudin 18.2 (CLDN18.2) is a tight-junction protein overexpressed and differently exposed in solid tumors such as gastric cancer (GCa), gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, and pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC).

evidence.claim: Serial PET imaging with 89Zr-labeled zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody) in mouse xenograft models (GSU, PATU8988S, HUPT-4) demonstrates high tumor uptake (mean 24.4–36.6 %ID/g on day 6) significantly exceeding control IgG (4.8 %ID/g; P=0.0029), directly confirming in vivo surface accessibility of CLDN18.2 ECD in solid tumors; supports zolbetuximab as a clinical-stage antibody engaging cell-surface CLDN18.2.

spans.quote: Serial PET imaging demonstrated a high tumor uptake of [89Zr]Zr-DFO-zolbetuximab at all 3 time points (mean uptake on day 6: 24.4 ± 7.8 %ID/g in GSU; 36.6 ± 10.1 %ID/g in PATU8988S; 16.48 ± 4.7 %ID/g in HUPT-4), and significantly higher than the uptake seen in mice imaged with [89Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG (day 6: 4.8 ± 1.9 %ID/g in GSU; P = 0.0029).

evidence.claim: Zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody) was radiolabeled with 89Zr (DFO conjugate) for serial PET imaging to measure in vivo tumor engagement; program demonstrates clinical-stage antibody (zolbetuximab, now FDA-approved for gastric/GEJ cancer) targeting CLDN18.2 extracellular domain at the cell surface.

spans.quote: Serial PET imaging with <sup>89</sup>Zr-labeled zolbetuximab, an anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody ([<sup>89</sup>Zr]Zr-DFO-zolbetuximab), and human IgG ([<sup>89</sup>Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG) as control (5.5-7.4 MBq) was performed 1, 3, and 6 d after injection, followed by ex vivo analysis of biodistribution.

evidence.claim: RNA sequencing confirms significantly higher expression of CLDN18.2 mRNA in human PDAC compared with noncancerous pancreas tissue, with no significant difference between treated and untreated tumors; provides RNA-level non-surface expression evidence qualifying the surface claims, though no surface-specific assay is performed in this clip.

spans.quote: Toxicity of [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-zolbetuximab was evaluated by laboratory and histologic analyses over 90 d. Results: RNA sequencing confirmed significantly higher expression of CLDN18.2 in human PDAC compared with noncancerous pancreas tissue, with no significant difference between treated and untreated tumors.

evidence.claim: Clinical discussion notes zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 mAb) and CAR-T therapies rely on cell-surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy; however, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, the exact subcellular localization of the fusion protein is uncertain, potentially compromising surface-directed therapies. This raises a contradictory/ambiguous scenario for CLDN18 surface accessibility in fusion-protein-positive tumors.

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 produces two tissue-restricted isoforms through alternative promoter usage: a lung-specific transcript (CLDN18.1) and a stomach-specific transcript (CLDN18.2). This is the foundational tissue-restriction datum for the gene.

spans.quote: Alternative usage of these promoters leads to production of lung and stomach-specific transcripts.

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 is expressed in lung tissue and is regulated by the NKX2.1 transcription factor; loss of NKX2.1 in mouse embryos causes down-regulation of claudin-18 in the lung, establishing lung as a primary tissue site of expression.

spans.quote: Here we describe the isolation and characterization of a novel target gene, termed claudin-18, that is down-regulated in the lungs of T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null mouse embryos.

evidence.claim: Among 11 claudin family members examined, only claudin-18 was selectively down-regulated in NKX2.1-null embryo lungs, confirming tissue-specific regulation and lung-restricted expression of this isoform.

spans.quote: Only claudin-18 was down-regulated in T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null embryo lungs among 11 claudin transcripts examined.

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 (isoform CLDN18.1) is normally expressed in alveolar epithelium and is a tight junction protein critical for maintaining alveolar integrity; alveolar epithelial cells are the primary normal-tissue cell type expressing this isoform.

spans.quote: Claudin-18 (Cldn18), a tight junction protein with a lung-specific isoform (Cldn18.1), is normally expressed in alveolar epithelium and is implicated in maintaining alveolar integrity.

evidence.claim: In normal lung, both normal alveoli and reactive pneumocytes consistently express Cldn18 at the tissue level. In contrast, lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD), especially non-mucinous LUAD, shows marked loss of Cldn18 expression relative to normal alveolar epithelium.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: normal alveoli, reactive pneumocytes, LUAD tumor cells

spans.quote: Normal alveoli and reactive pneumocytes consistently expressed Cldn18, whereas LUADs, especially non-mucinous LUADs (NM-LUADs), typically showed marked loss.

evidence.claim: Disease-state modulation: CLDN18 expression is completely absent in 83% of non-mucinous LUAD resections and 84.8% of biopsies, representing a near-total loss of surface-accessible CLDN18.1 in this tumor subtype relative to normal alveolar epithelium. This is a strong refutation of surface accessibility in non-mucinous LUAD.

spans.quote: Complete absence of Cldn18 was observed in 83% of NM-LUAD resections and 84.8% of NM-LUAD biopsies.

evidence.claim: Lepidic non-mucinous LUAD components show low CLDN18 expression in the majority of cases (>50% of tumor cells showing low expression in 24/26 cases), indicating that even the less aggressive LUAD growth pattern retains loss of CLDN18.1 surface expression.

spans.quote: Lepidic NM-LUAD components, a frequent diagnostic pitfall especially in small specimens, showed low expression in most cases (>50% of tumour cells in 24/26).

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 expression in normal tissue is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa; it is absent from the gastric stem cell zone. This cell-state restriction (differentiated vs. stem/progenitor cells) is a defining feature of normal CLDN18.2 tissue biology.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: differentiated gastric mucosal epithelial cells and gastric stem cell zone

spans.quote: Its expression in normal tissues is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa, but it is absent from the gastric stem cell zone.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 is retained upon malignant transformation of gastric mucosa and is expressed in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and their metastases, indicating that disease-state transition from normal to malignant gastric epithelium preserves rather than abolishes surface-accessible CLDN18.2.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: primary gastric cancer tumor cells and metastatic lesions

spans.quote: CLDN18.2 is retained on malignant transformation and is expressed in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and the metastases thereof.

evidence.claim: Ectopic CLDN18.2 activation occurs frequently in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors, correlating with distinct histologic subtypes. This represents disease-state-induced ectopic expression of a normally stomach-restricted isoform across multiple tumor types.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumor cells

spans.quote: In addition to its orthotopic expression, we found frequent ectopic activation of CLDN18.2 in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors, correlating with distinct histologic subtypes.

evidence.claim: IHC pan-cancer survey (MONSTAR-SCREEN-2) found CLDN18.2 positivity (≥40% tumor cells with any staining intensity) in 16.3% of patients overall, with highest prevalence in gastric (54.5%), biliary tract (21.7%), pancreatic (20.7%), and small intestinal (18.2%) cancers.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: multi-cancer tumor cohort (gastric, biliary, pancreatic, small intestinal)

spans.quote: IHC detected CLDN18.2 (≥ 40% of tumor cells showing any staining intensity) in 16.3% of patients, with highest prevalence in gastric (54.5%), biliary tract (21.7%), pancreatic (20.7%), and small intestinal (18.2%) cancers.

evidence.claim: Whole transcriptome sequencing (WTS) in 2191 patients identified CLDN18-high tumors in 13.8% overall, with highest proportions in gastric (64.5%), small intestinal (40.0%), pancreatic (37.8%), and biliary tract (20.0%) cancers, confirming multi-cancer RNA-level expression.

spans.quote: WTS analysis with optimized transcript thresholds (n = 2191) demonstrated the CLDN18-high population to be 13.8%, with highest proportions in gastric (64.5%), small intestinal (40.0%), pancreatic (37.8%), and biliary tract (20.0%) cancers.

evidence.claim: Isoform analysis of 364 solid tumor patients revealed strong CLDN18.2 predominance (mean 18.2/18.1 proportion 0.945); CLDN18.1 predominance was observed in only 4.9% of patients. This quantifies the isoform balance in the disease context.

spans.quote: Isoform analysis of 364 patients revealed CLDN18.2 predominance (mean 18.2/18.1 proportion 0.945), with CLDN18.1 predominance observed in only 4.9% of patients.

evidence.claim: Using a 5% IHC cut-off, CLDN18 positivity was detected in 92% of gastric-type adenocarcinomas (GAS) of the uterine cervix, 0% of non-gastric-type adenocarcinomas, and only 7% of HPV-associated endocervical adenocarcinomas (ECAs), demonstrating strict histological-subtype-dependent expression in cervical tumors.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: gastric-type endocervical adenocarcinoma, non-gastric adenocarcinoma, HPV-associated ECA tumor cells

spans.quote: When a 5% cut-off was used, CLDN18 positivity was detected in 22/24 (92%) GASs, 0/11 (0%) non-GASs and 6/86 (7%) HPV-associated ECAs; CLDN18 expression was thus significantly associated with GAS histology (P < 0.0001).

evidence.claim: Using a stringent IHC cut-off (≥75% tumor cells staining), CLDN18 positivity was detected in 33% of gastric-type adenocarcinomas of the cervix, 0% of non-gastric subtypes, and only 2% of HPV-associated ECAs, demonstrating high isoform-selective tissue restriction even in ectopic tumor settings.

spans.quote: CLDN18 positivity was detected in 8/24 (33%) GASs, 0/11 (0%) non-GASs and 2/86 (2%) HPV-associated ECAs, with positivity defined as staining in ≥75% of tumour cells, as in gastric cancer.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 expression (any staining) was observed in 29.5% of biliary tract cancer (BTC) samples; stringent positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous in ≥75% tumor cells) was found in only 5.5% (13/237). This quantifies the low but non-zero surface prevalence in BTC.

spans.quote: Overall, CLDN18 expression was observed in 70/237 BTCs (29.5%) samples, while CLDN18 positivity (i.e. moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of the tumor cells) was found in 13/237 BTCs (5.5%).

evidence.claim: PDAC patient eligibility criteria based on CLDN18 membranous IHC expression (moderate-to-strong in ≥75% tumor cells) confirms that CLDN18 protein is present at the membrane of PDAC tumor cells in a clinically meaningful fraction of patients, supporting PDAC as a surface-accessible context.

spans.quote: Eligible patients had tumors expressing CLDN18, with moderate to strong membranous expression by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in ≥75 % tumor cells[ 16 , 17 ].

evidence.claim: IHC protein expression of CLDN18 was assessed in representative PDAC, chronic pancreatitis (CP), normal pancreatic, duodenal, and liver tissue sections, providing a comparative normal-versus-disease tissue panel for CLDN18 membrane protein expression in the pancreatic axis.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC, chronic pancreatitis, normal pancreas, duodenum, liver tissue sections

spans.quote: The protein expressions of CEACAM5, TMPRSS4, COL17A1, CLDN18, and AQP5 in representative PDAC, CP, pancreatic, duodenal, and liver tissue sections are shown in Figure 1 .

evidence.claim: A corroborating study found CLDN18 expression almost exclusively in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung cancer tissues, consistent with ectopic tumor-restricted expression of CLDN18.2 outside the normal stomach context.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, lung cancer tissues

spans.quote: Another study corroborated that CLDN18 was expressed almost exclusively in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung cancer tissues ( Sahin et al., 2008 ).

evidence.claim: Transcriptomic analysis of ovarian tumor versus normal tissue identified CLDN18 as an adhesion protein with the highest differential expression between ovarian tumors and normal tissues, supporting ectopic disease-state-induced expression in ovarian cancer.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: ovarian tumor versus normal ovarian tissue

spans.quote: The final set of genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with the highest difference between ovarian tumors and normal tissues consisted of membrane receptors (TFRC, OPRK1, EBAG9, HTR5A, GABRB1, and CD47), adhesion proteins (EpCAM, CLDN18, and CLDN1), and molecule transporters (KCNG1, KCNH2, and SLC22A11) ( Figure 1E ).

evidence.claim: Multi-omic surfaceome profiling of PDAC confirmed CLDN18 as an established therapeutic target with differential expression between PDAC tumor and normal tissues; alongside MSLN, TROP2, MUC1, and CEACAM5, with low or minimal expression in most toxicity-prone normal tissues.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC tumor versus normal tissue panel

spans.quote: Transcriptional and proteomic investigation of the cell surfaceome revealed established therapeutic targets ( CLDN18 , MSLN , TROP2 , MUC1 , CEACAM5 ) and discovered additional PDAC antigens ( GPRC5A , TM4SF1 ) with low or minimal expression in most toxicity-prone normal tissues.

evidence.claim: Proteomic surfaceome analysis of adenocarcinoma of the esophagogastric junction (AEG/GEJ) tumors versus paired normal adjacent tissue (NAT) showed CLDN18 among established ADC targets with measurable log2 fold-change increases in tumor versus precancerous GEJ tissues.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AEG/GEJ tumor and paired normal adjacent tissue

spans.quote: A Heatmap of top 20 DEPs between AEG tumor and paired NAT samples; B Venn diagram of top 20 DEPs and membrane proteins from Cancer Surfaceome Atlas; C heatmap of the normal tissue expression index of eight candidate targets; D bar chart of log 2 fold-change of established ADC targets (HER2, TROP2, and CLDN18) and CD66c in the tumor and precancerous tissues in each GEJ patient; E heatmap of clinicopathological characteristics among different CD66c expression level; F box plots compare CD66c protein expression quantities in different Siewert types

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 protein is a constituent of tight junction strands in epithelial cells, establishing its localization at tight junctions of epithelial cell-cell contacts as the primary subcellular compartment in normal tissue.

spans.quote: The gene product exhibits an amino acid sequence similar to those of the claudin multigene family of proteins that constitute tight junction strands in epithelial cells.

evidence.claim: HPA database annotates CLDN18 subcellular localization as Cell Junctions, confirming tight junction membrane subdomain assignment consistent with claudin family biology.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Cell Junctions.

evidence.claim: HPA flags CLDN18 as a junctional protein, confirming tight junction membrane subdomain localization in the HPA database annotation.

spans.quote: HPA flags this gene as junctional.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry of intact (non-permeabilized) N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 confirmed surface expression of both isoforms using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody, demonstrating plasma membrane accessibility of the extracellular domains of both isoforms in a gastric cancer cell line model.

spans.quote: Left, Flow cytometry analysis of CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 surface expression in N87 cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody recognizing both isoforms.

evidence.claim: In the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion (a chromosomal rearrangement found in gastric cancer), the exact subcellular localization of the fusion protein is uncertain, raising ambiguity about whether the CLDN18 extracellular domains remain surface-accessible for therapeutic antibodies (e.g. zolbetuximab) or CAR-T cells targeting cell-surface CLDN18.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: gastric cancer tumor cells with CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: Surfaceome proteomics data places CLDN18 in the 'Membrane' subcellular localization category in PDAC, with a tumor/normal expression ratio of 2.1 (64.4 vs 133.3 normalized protein abundance), indicating membrane-localized CLDN18 protein is detectable in PDAC tumor tissue.

evidence.claim: In CD-SBN (CD-type small bowel-type borderline neoplasms, a specialized intestinal metaplasia context), approximately half of CDH17-positive lesions co-expressed CLDN18 with membranous staining alongside gastric-type mucins MUC5AC and MUC6, indicating CLDN18 membranous expression in this metaplastic gastric-phenotype context.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: CD-SBN intestinal metaplasia lesions with gastric phenotype

spans.quote: Interestingly, we found 92% of CD-SBNs were CDH17 positive and 91% of them showed ≥ 40% extent with moderate or strong membranous expression, and approximately half of them co-expressed gastric-type mucins, such as MUC5AC and MUC6 with frequently CLDN18.

confidence_reasoning: A1 grade is direct_multi_method with only two low-to-moderate contradictions (IHC scoring ambiguity at low severity; CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion uncertainty at moderate severity, affecting a minor tumor subset). A2 confirms high state dependence (junctional sequestration in normal tissue resolved by tumor transformation) but does not introduce a secreted or dominant intracellular form. The therapeutic engagement block includes an FDA-approved antibody with in vivo PET validation of ECD accessibility. Together, the strong direct evidence base, the absence of shedding or secreted decoy, and the biological explanation for state dependence support high confidence, with state_dependence flagged as high to communicate the context-dependency to downstream users.
